title: Marbella New Year's Eve Yacht Charter 2026: Costs, Boats and Tips
html:
If you are planning to celebrate the turn of 2026 in style, a yacht charter for New Year's Eve in Marbella offers a peerless way to watch the fireworks over Puerto Banús. With a sunset cruise from the old port at Puerto Deportivo de Marbella, expect to pay from EUR 1,500 for a shared tour to EUR 8,000 for a private crewed superyacht. BoatHire24 connects you directly with verified owners to help you find the perfect vessel.

How much does it cost to rent a yacht in Marbella for New Year's Eve?

Charter prices for New Year's Eve in Marbella range from EUR 1,200 for a half-day small motorboat to EUR 10,000 for a fully crewed 60-foot superyacht. The premium for this peak night is typically 20-30% above standard rates. Many operators require a minimum four-hour hire. You can book a charter online via the BoatHire24 platform here: book a charter online in a few clicks.

Where to set off in Marbella

Marbella's coastline offers several well-equipped departure points. Puerto Banús is the most famous, with its designer boutiques and superyacht berths. The public harbours of Puerto Deportivo de Marbella and Cabopino port are quieter and often more affordable. To the east, the fishing village of La Carihuela in Torremolinos and the sheltered bay of Fuengirola are also popular. For a more secluded start, consider the small marina of Estepona, just a 20-minute drive west of Marbella.

Which boat to rent

  1. Small motorboat (up to 30 ft), best for a couple's private cruise; up to 6 guests; from EUR 1,200 for half a day.
  2. Day cruiser or sports boat (30-40 ft), good for families; up to 10 guests; from EUR 2,500 half-day.
  3. Catamaran (40-50 ft), stable and spacious for groups; up to 12 guests; from EUR 3,500 full-day.
  4. Motor yacht (50-70 ft), ideal for a party with friends; up to 16 guests; from EUR 5,000 full-day.
  5. Luxury superyacht (70 ft+), crewed including captain and steward for a VIP night; up to 20 guests; from EUR 8,000 for an evening charter.

For a dependable model, consider the Princess F70 for hire, a 72-foot motor yacht that sleeps eight and cruises comfortably at 28 knots.

How much does a crewed catamaran cost for New Year's Eve?

A full-day crewed catamaran charter on 31 December 2026 typically starts at EUR 4,000 for a Lagoon 46, including skipper, fuel and basic catering. The price can rise to EUR 7,000 for a larger catamaran with a full crew and champagne dinner. Many charter companies require a deposit of 50% at booking.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Marbella?

To skipper a motorboat over 15 HP (approximately 11 kW) in Spain you need a leisure boating licence, but bareboat charters often include a licensed skipper. Spain requires the 'Licencia de Navegación' for boats up to 8 metres, while larger vessels demand the 'Patrón de Navegación Básica'. The operator on BoatHire24 will confirm the exact requirements for your chosen vessel, so you never need to worry.

A local tip: New Year's Eve in Marbella brings a parade of superyachts in Puerto Banús at 11 pm. Position your boat near the jetty to watch the firework display at midnight.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 1,200, EUR 10,000
  • Peak months: December, especially 31st
  • Typical trip length: 4, 6 hours
  • Capacity: 6, 20 guests
  • Currency: EUR
  • Nearest airport: Málaga (AGP), 40 km east

To find your ideal boat, use the boat search on BoatHire24. Filter by date, location and boat type to see real-time availability and prices.

Questions about boat rental in Marbella

Can I rent a yacht for just the evening of New Year's Eve?

Yes, many owners offer evening charters from 6 pm to midnight, costing around 70% of the full-day rate.

Is it possible to book a last-minute charter for New Year's Eve 2026?

It is risky; most boats are reserved by mid-December, but cancellations sometimes appear on the marketplace.

What happens if the weather is bad on New Year's Eve in Marbella?

Charter operators typically cancel or reschedule to the next day if the forecast shows dangerous seas, and you would receive a full refund.

Are there any special restrictions for party boats on New Year's Eve?

Spanish maritime law prohibits queuing and loud music after 2 am in port; ask your skipper about anchorage spots outside the marina.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
